name: Buche De Noel (Christmas Log Cake)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a jelly roll baking sheet and line with parchment paper.
2. Sift dry ingredients together.
3. Beat eggs at high speed for about 5 to 10 minutes.
4. Add sugar by tablespoonfuls, continuing to beat until the butter is very thick.
5. Add lemon juice and water; fold in dry ingredients in four stages (1/4 cup each time).
6. Spread evenly on the baking sheet and bake at 375°F for 15 minutes or until the sponge springs back when tested.
7. Sift powdered sugar onto the sponge. Turn cake out onto a clean tea towel, remove parchment paper, and trim edges if crispy.
8. Roll up gently while still warm, leaving the towel inside.
9. Let cool completely.
10. Beat egg whites, then add cream of tartar; beat until foamy.
11. Gradually beat in sugar until meringue is stiff and glossy.
12. Pipe meringue through a pastry bag to form equal numbers of stems and caps resembling small mushrooms.
13. Bake mushroom pieces at 250°F for about 45 minutes.
14. Remove from oven and glue the pieces together with buttercream icing; dust lightly with cocoa using a fine strainer.
15. Beat egg yolks and butter until smooth, add espresso powder and milk, then gradually add confectioners' sugar. Mix about 1/3 to 1/2 cup with green food coloring for ivy decoration.
16. Assemble by unrolling the cooled sponge, spreading it onto a serving plate, frosting with buttercream icing, cutting off a diagonal slice (about 2-inch), and frosting the outside. Add the stump and frost again. Create ivy patterns and add mushrooms. Dust the entire cake lightly with powdered sugar to simulate snow.
17. Refrigerate until ready to serve; cut with a serrated knife.
